codeReview.summary: Implementation is complete with admin login, dashboard with platform stats, user management (paginated list with search, user detail view), suspend/activate user via PATCH /api/v1/admin/users/{id}/status, RBAC middleware, and 30-min access token expiry. Frontend pages for /admin/login, /admin, /admin/users, /admin/users/{id} are all implemented. Admin tokens stored in localStorage.

issues.description: Dashboard stats `reports_count`, `total_lessons_completed`, `total_tests_taken` are hardcoded to 0. These should query actual database tables to return real counts.
Admin dashboard returns empty `recent_activity` list as a comment says 'Placeholder for activity feed'. The schema defines it as `list` but frontend doesn't display anything for it.

Badges section shows placeholder text 'Badge system coming soon'. This was documented as a known issue in discoveredIssues but the feature description says 'User detail: profile, activity history, subscriptions, badges'.

Admin tokens stored in localStorage. Architecture.md (in .factory/library/) documents that 'Frontend stores tokens in memory (not localStorage for security)'. Storing admin tokens in localStorage exposes them to XSS attacks, unlike httpOnly cookies.

Token refresh logic uses regular `/auth/refresh` endpoint. For admin sessions with 30-min expiry, if an admin token expires, the refresh will succeed but return a regular user token, not an admin token. Admin-specific refresh handling may be needed.

Admin layout checks localStorage for token on mount but doesn't handle token expiry mid-session. If the admin token expires after 30 minutes of inactivity while the admin is still using the panel, API calls will fail with 401 and redirect to /login, but without a clear message about session expiry.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Admin tokens stored in localStorage instead of httpOnly cookies. Architecture.md explicitly says 'Frontend stores tokens in memory (not localStorage for security)'. The regular user auth uses httpOnly cookies for refresh tokens, but admin uses localStorage. This is an inconsistency with documented patterns.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: apps/web/src/lib/api/client.ts:14-20 - admin token check uses localStorage.getItem('admin_access_token'). Also in apps/web/src/app/admin/login/page.tsx:42 stores admin_access_token in localStorage.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Frontend-worker skill says to use agent-browser for UI validation after implementing components. The transcript shows no agent-browser invocations. Verification was limited to typecheck, lint, and py_compile. The handoff's skillFeedback.followedProcedure is true despite this deviation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: SKILL.md frontend-worker says: 'Open in browser via agent-browser and verify: Page renders without errors, All interactive elements work, Mobile viewport (375px) displays correctly, No console errors'. Handoff shows only typecheck, lint, py_compile commands ran. No interactive checks documented.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker hardcoded placeholder values (0 for reports_count, total_lessons_completed, total_tests_taken) instead of querying actual tables. This suggests the worker may not have known about the existence of reports, lesson_progress, or test_attempts tables, or how to query them. The seed-ssc-gd-content feature was supposed to create these tables.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: apps/api/internal/admin/service.py:107-111 - reports_count = 0, total_lessons_completed = 0, total_tests_taken = 0 with comments 'Placeholder - would come from reports table' etc.

summary: The basic-admin-panel feature is implemented and covers all specified functionality. Admin login, dashboard, user list (paginated/search), user detail, and suspend/activate user are all working. The main blocking issue is that dashboard stats for reports, lessons completed, and tests taken are hardcoded to 0 rather than being real queries. Non-blocking issues include localStorage token storage (deviating from architecture docs), lack of agent-browser validation per skill procedure, and placeholder badges UI.
